concepts.label: Atom
concepts.definition: The smallest unit of a chemical element that retains its chemical properties

concepts.label: Molecule
concepts.definition: A group of atoms bonded together, representing the smallest fundamental unit of a chemical compound

concepts.label: Element
concepts.definition: A pure substance consisting of only one type of atom

concepts.label: Compound
concepts.definition: A substance formed when two or more elements are chemically bonded together

concepts.label: Chemical Reaction
concepts.definition: A process in which one or more substances are converted into different substances

concepts.label: Catalyst
concepts.definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed

concepts.label: pH
concepts.definition: A scale used to specify the acidity or basicity of an aqueous solution

concepts.label: Acid
concepts.definition: A substance that donates hydrogen ions (H+) in aqueous solution

concepts.label: Base
concepts.definition: A substance that accepts hydrogen ions (H+) or donates hydroxide ions (OH-) in aqueous solution

concepts.label: Oxidation
concepts.definition: A chemical process in which a substance loses electrons or increases in oxidation state

concepts.label: Reduction
concepts.definition: A chemical process in which a substance gains electrons or decreases in oxidation state

concepts.label: Ion
concepts.definition: An atom or molecule that has gained or lost one or more electrons, giving it a net electric charge

concepts.label: Cation
concepts.definition: A positively charged ion

concepts.label: Anion
concepts.definition: A negatively charged ion

concepts.label: Ionic Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond formed by the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions

concepts.label: Covalent Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond formed by the sharing of electron pairs between atoms

concepts.label: Metallic Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond formed by the delocalization of electrons in a 'sea' around metal cations

concepts.label: Hydrogen Bond
concepts.definition: A weak attractive force between a hydrogen atom and an electronegative atom

concepts.label: Van der Waals Forces
concepts.definition: Weak intermolecular forces arising from induced dipoles and London dispersion forces

concepts.label: Electronegativity
concepts.definition: The ability of an atom to attract electrons in a chemical bond

concepts.label: Polarity
concepts.definition: The distribution of electric charge in a molecule resulting in partial positive and negative regions

concepts.label: Dipole
concepts.definition: A molecule or chemical group having two poles with opposite electric charges

concepts.label: Solubility
concepts.definition: The ability of a substance to dissolve in a solvent to form a homogeneous solution

concepts.label: Solution
concepts.definition: A homogeneous mixture of two or more substances

concepts.label: Solvent
concepts.definition: The substance in which another substance (solute) is dissolved to form a solution

concepts.label: Solute
concepts.definition: The substance that is dissolved in a solvent to form a solution

concepts.label: Concentration
concepts.definition: The amount of solute present in a given amount of solution or solvent

concepts.label: Molarity
concepts.definition: The number of moles of solute per liter of solution

concepts.label: Mole
concepts.definition: A unit of measurement equal to 6.022 × 10²³ particles (Avogadro's number)

concepts.label: Avogadro's Number
concepts.definition: The number of particles in one mole of a substance, approximately 6.022 × 10²³

concepts.label: Atomic Mass
concepts.definition: The mass of an atom expressed in atomic mass units

concepts.label: Molecular Weight
concepts.definition: The sum of the atomic weights of all atoms in a molecule

concepts.label: Stoichiometry
concepts.definition: The calculation of quantities in chemical reactions based on balanced chemical equations

concepts.label: Chemical Equation
concepts.definition: A symbolic representation of a chemical reaction showing reactants and products

concepts.label: Reactant
concepts.definition: A substance that participates in a chemical reaction and is consumed to form products

concepts.label: Product
concepts.definition: A substance that is formed as a result of a chemical reaction

concepts.label: Equilibrium
concepts.definition: A state in which the rates of forward and reverse reactions are equal

concepts.label: Le Chatelier's Principle
concepts.definition: When a system at equilibrium is disturbed, it shifts to counteract the disturbance

concepts.label: Activation Energy
concepts.definition: The minimum energy required for a chemical reaction to occur

concepts.label: Enthalpy
concepts.definition: A measure of the total heat content of a thermodynamic system

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: A measure of the disorder or randomness in a system

concepts.label: Gibbs Free Energy
concepts.definition: A thermodynamic quantity that determines the spontaneity of a process

concepts.label: Endothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: A chemical reaction that absorbs heat from the surroundings

concepts.label: Exothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: A chemical reaction that releases heat to the surroundings

concepts.label: Periodic Table
concepts.definition: A tabular arrangement of chemical elements organized by atomic number and recurring properties

concepts.label: Atomic Number
concepts.definition: The number of protons in the nucleus of an atom

concepts.label: Mass Number
concepts.definition: The total number of protons and neutrons in an atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Isotope
concepts.definition: Atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons

concepts.label: Electron Configuration
concepts.definition: The distribution of electrons in atomic or molecular orbitals

concepts.label: Orbital
concepts.definition: A mathematical function describing the wave-like behavior of an electron in an atom

concepts.label: Valence Electrons
concepts.definition: Electrons in the outermost shell of an atom that participate in chemical bonding

concepts.label: Octet Rule
concepts.definition: Atoms tend to form bonds to achieve eight electrons in their valence shell

concepts.label: Lewis Structure
concepts.definition: A diagram showing the bonding between atoms and lone pairs in a molecule

concepts.label: VSEPR Theory
concepts.definition: Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion theory predicts molecular geometry

concepts.label: Hybridization
concepts.definition: The concept of mixing atomic orbitals to form new hybrid orbitals

concepts.label: Resonance
concepts.definition: The existence of multiple valid Lewis structures for a molecule

concepts.label: Formal Charge
concepts.definition: A theoretical charge assigned to an atom in a molecule assuming equal electron sharing

concepts.label: Oxidation State
concepts.definition: The hypothetical charge of an atom if all bonds were completely ionic

concepts.label: Redox Reaction
concepts.definition: A chemical reaction involving the transfer of electrons between species

concepts.label: Half-Reaction
concepts.definition: Either the oxidation or reduction component of a redox reaction

concepts.label: Electrochemistry
concepts.definition: The study of chemical processes that cause electrons to move

concepts.label: Galvanic Cell
concepts.definition: An electrochemical cell that generates electrical energy from spontaneous redox reactions

concepts.label: Electrolytic Cell
concepts.definition: An electrochemical cell that uses electrical energy to drive non-spontaneous reactions

concepts.label: Electrode
concepts.definition: A conductor through which electricity enters or leaves an electrolyte

concepts.label: Anode
concepts.definition: The electrode where oxidation occurs in an electrochemical cell

concepts.label: Cathode
concepts.definition: The electrode where reduction occurs in an electrochemical cell

concepts.label: Electrolyte
concepts.definition: A substance that conducts electricity through the movement of ions

concepts.label: Standard Electrode Potential
concepts.definition: The potential of an electrode under standard conditions relative to the standard hydrogen electrode

concepts.label: Nernst Equation
concepts.definition: An equation relating the electrode potential to the concentrations of reactants and products

concepts.label: Faraday's Law
concepts.definition: The amount of substance produced at an electrode is proportional to the quantity of electricity

concepts.label: Phase
concepts.definition: A distinct physical state of matter with uniform properties throughout

concepts.label: Phase Transition
concepts.definition: The change of a substance from one phase to another

concepts.label: Melting Point
concepts.definition: The temperature at which a solid changes to a liquid

concepts.label: Boiling Point
concepts.definition: The temperature at which a liquid changes to a gas

concepts.label: Vapor Pressure
concepts.definition: The pressure exerted by vapor molecules above a liquid surface

concepts.label: Colligative Properties
concepts.definition: Properties that depend on the number of solute particles, not their identity

concepts.label: Osmotic Pressure
concepts.definition: The pressure needed to stop the flow of solvent across a semipermeable membrane

concepts.label: Osmosis
concepts.definition: The movement of solvent through a semipermeable membrane toward higher solute concentration

concepts.label: Diffusion
concepts.definition: The movement of particles from high to low concentration

concepts.label: Effusion
concepts.definition: The escape of gas molecules through a small opening

concepts.label: Graham's Law
concepts.definition: The rate of effusion of gases is inversely proportional to the square root of their molar masses

concepts.label: Ideal Gas Law
concepts.definition: A mathematical relationship between pressure, volume, temperature, and amount of gas (PV = nRT)

concepts.label: Real Gas
concepts.definition: A gas whose behavior deviates from the ideal gas law due to intermolecular forces

concepts.label: Van der Waals Equation
concepts.definition: An equation of state that accounts for the non-ideal behavior of real gases

concepts.label: Critical Point
concepts.definition: The temperature and pressure above which liquid and gas phases are indistinguishable

concepts.label: Triple Point
concepts.definition: The temperature and pressure at which all three phases of a substance coexist

concepts.label: Phase Diagram
concepts.definition: A graph showing the phases of a substance under different conditions of temperature and pressure

concepts.label: Sublimation
concepts.definition: The direct transition from solid to gas phase without passing through liquid

concepts.label: Deposition
concepts.definition: The direct transition from gas to solid phase without passing through liquid

concepts.label: Crystal Lattice
concepts.definition: The regular arrangement of atoms, ions, or molecules in a crystalline solid

concepts.label: Unit Cell
concepts.definition: The smallest repeating unit in a crystal lattice

concepts.label: Allotrope
concepts.definition: Different structural forms of the same element

concepts.label: Isomer
concepts.definition: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements

concepts.label: Structural Isomer
concepts.definition: Isomers with different connectivity of atoms

concepts.label: Stereoisomer
concepts.definition: Isomers with the same connectivity but different spatial arrangement

concepts.label: Enantiomer
concepts.definition: Stereoisomers that are non-superimposable mirror images of each other

concepts.label: Chirality
concepts.definition: The property of a molecule having non-superimposable mirror images

concepts.label: Optical Activity
concepts.definition: The ability of a chiral compound to rotate plane-polarized light

concepts.label: Functional Group
concepts.definition: A specific arrangement of atoms that gives characteristic properties to organic compounds

concepts.label: Hydrocarbon
concepts.definition: A compound consisting entirely of hydrogen and carbon atoms

concepts.label: Alkane
concepts.definition: A saturated hydrocarbon with only single bonds between carbon atoms

concepts.label: Alkene
concepts.definition: An unsaturated hydrocarbon containing at least one carbon-carbon double bond

concepts.label: Alkyne
concepts.definition: An unsaturated hydrocarbon containing at least one carbon-carbon triple bond

concepts.label: Aromatic Compound
concepts.definition: A cyclic compound with delocalized π electrons following Hückel's rule

concepts.label: Benzene
concepts.definition: The simplest aromatic hydrocarbon with the formula C₆H₆

concepts.label: Alcohol
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a hydroxyl (-OH) functional group

concepts.label: Aldehyde
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a carbonyl group bonded to hydrogen

concepts.label: Ketone
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a carbonyl group bonded to two carbon atoms

concepts.label: Carboxylic Acid
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a carboxyl (-COOH) functional group

concepts.label: Ester
concepts.definition: An organic compound formed by the reaction of a carboxylic acid and an alcohol

concepts.label: Amine
concepts.definition: An organic compound derived from ammonia with one or more hydrogen atoms replaced by alkyl groups

concepts.label: Amide
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a carbonyl group linked to a nitrogen atom

concepts.label: Ether
concepts.definition: An organic compound in which two hydrocarbon groups are linked by an oxygen atom

concepts.label: Polymer
concepts.definition: A large molecule composed of repeated subunits called monomers

concepts.label: Monomer
concepts.definition: A small molecule that can be bonded to other identical molecules to form a polymer

concepts.label: Polymerization
concepts.definition: The process of combining monomers to form polymers

concepts.label: Addition Polymerization
concepts.definition: Polymerization where monomers add together without eliminating small molecules

concepts.label: Condensation Polymerization
concepts.definition: Polymerization involving the elimination of small molecules like water

concepts.label: Nucleophile
concepts.definition: A chemical species that donates an electron pair to form a chemical bond

concepts.label: Electrophile
concepts.definition: A chemical species that accepts an electron pair to form a chemical bond

concepts.label: Substitution Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction where one functional group is replaced by another

concepts.label: Addition Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction where atoms or groups are added to a molecule without removing other atoms

concepts.label: Elimination Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction where atoms or groups are removed from a molecule

concepts.label: Oxidation Number
concepts.definition: The formal charge assigned to an atom in a compound based on electronegativity

concepts.label: Buffer
concepts.definition: A solution that resists changes in pH when small amounts of acid or base are added

concepts.label: Henderson-Hasselbalch Equation
concepts.definition: An equation relating pH to the ratio of conjugate base to acid concentrations

concepts.label: Conjugate Acid-Base Pair
concepts.definition: Two species that differ by the transfer of a single proton

concepts.label: Brønsted-Lowry Acid
concepts.definition: A proton donor in acid-base reactions

concepts.label: Brønsted-Lowry Base
concepts.definition: A proton acceptor in acid-base reactions

concepts.label: Lewis Acid
concepts.definition: An electron pair acceptor

concepts.label: Lewis Base
concepts.definition: An electron pair donor

concepts.label: Amphoteric
concepts.definition: A substance that can act as both an acid and a base

concepts.label: Titration
concepts.definition: A technique used to determine the concentration of a solution by reaction with a standard solution

concepts.label: Equivalence Point
concepts.definition: The point in a titration where stoichiometrically equivalent amounts of reactants have been mixed

concepts.label: End Point
concepts.definition: The point in a titration where the indicator changes, approximating the equivalence point

concepts.label: Indicator
concepts.definition: A substance that changes color at or near the equivalence point of a titration

concepts.label: Solubility Product
concepts.definition: The equilibrium constant for the dissolution of a sparingly soluble ionic compound

concepts.label: Common Ion Effect
concepts.definition: The reduction in solubility of an ionic compound due to the presence of a common ion

concepts.label: Precipitation
concepts.definition: The formation of an insoluble solid from a solution

concepts.label: Complex Ion
concepts.definition: A charged species consisting of a central metal ion bonded to ligands

concepts.label: Ligand
concepts.definition: A molecule or ion that forms coordinate bonds with a central metal atom

concepts.label: Coordination Number
concepts.definition: The number of coordinate bonds formed by the central metal ion in a complex

concepts.label: Crystal Field Theory
concepts.definition: A theory describing the electronic structure of transition metal complexes

concepts.label: d-Orbital Splitting
concepts.definition: The energy difference between d-orbitals in transition metal complexes due to ligand field effects

concepts.label: Transition Metal
concepts.definition: An element whose atom has a partially filled d subshell or can form cations with partially filled d subshells

concepts.label: Lanthanide
concepts.definition: Any of the 15 elements from lanthanum to lutetium in the periodic table

concepts.label: Actinide
concepts.definition: Any of the 15 elements from actinium to lawrencium in the periodic table

concepts.label: Noble Gas
concepts.definition: Elements in group 18 of the periodic table with complete valence shells

concepts.label: Halogen
concepts.definition: Elements in group 17 of the periodic table with seven valence electrons

concepts.label: Alkali Metal
concepts.definition: Elements in group 1 of the periodic table with one valence electron

concepts.label: Alkaline Earth Metal
concepts.definition: Elements in group 2 of the periodic table with two valence electrons

concepts.label: Metalloid
concepts.definition: Elements with properties intermediate between metals and nonmetals

concepts.label: Alloy
concepts.definition: A mixture of two or more elements, at least one of which is a metal

concepts.label: Interstitial Compound
concepts.definition: A compound formed when small atoms occupy the interstices in a metal lattice

concepts.label: Nonstoichiometric Compound
concepts.definition: A compound that does not have a fixed ratio of elements

concepts.label: Defect Structure
concepts.definition: Imperfections in crystal lattices that affect material properties

concepts.label: Superconductor
concepts.definition: A material that conducts electricity with zero electrical resistance below a critical temperature

concepts.label: Semiconductor
concepts.definition: A material with electrical conductivity between that of conductors and insulators

concepts.label: Band Theory
concepts.definition: A theory explaining the electronic properties of solids through energy bands

concepts.label: Valence Band
concepts.definition: The highest energy band that is completely filled with electrons at absolute zero

concepts.label: Conduction Band
concepts.definition: The lowest energy band that is empty at absolute zero

concepts.label: Band Gap
concepts.definition: The energy difference between the valence and conduction bands

concepts.label: Doping
concepts.definition: The introduction of impurities into a pure semiconductor to modify its properties

concepts.label: p-Type Semiconductor
concepts.definition: A semiconductor doped with electron-accepting impurities

concepts.label: n-Type Semiconductor
concepts.definition: A semiconductor doped with electron-donating impurities

concepts.label: p-n Junction
concepts.definition: The boundary between p-type and n-type semiconductor materials
